content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS consisting of only ((( and ))).

Find the length of the **longest** substring of SSS which is a valid parentheses string.

Note:


A substring of a string is obtained by deleting some (possibly zero) characters from the beginning and some (possibly zero) characters from the end.
A valid parentheses string is defined as:

Empty string is valid.
If PPP is valid, (P)(P)(P) is also valid.
If PPP and QQQ are valid, PQPQPQ is also valid.



Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case consists of a string SSS, consisting of ((( and ))) only.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the length of the **longest** substring of SSS which is a valid parentheses string.
constraints: The sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The longest valid parentheses substring is ()()() having length 222.
Test case 222: There is no valid parentheses substring in the string.
Test case 333: The longest valid parentheses substring is (())()(())()(())() having length 666.
Test case 444: There is no valid parentheses substring in the string.
